framework_bugs.summary: init.sh platform module copy filename mismatch (mcp_server vs mcp-server)
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash init.sh --non-interactive --project x --platform mcp_server --deployment organizational --gov-mode private_poc --language python --project-dir /tmp/x --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private --allow-existing-dir; ls /tmp/x/docs/platform-modules/
framework_bugs.expected: docs/platform-modules/ contains the mcp_server platform module so verify-install.sh does not emit a manual-action warning.
framework_bugs.actual: docs/platform-modules/ is empty. init.sh:1124 looks for $SCRIPT_DIR/docs/platform-modules/${PLATFORM}.md (i.e. mcp_server.md) but the framework actually ships docs/platform-modules/mcp-server.md (hyphenated). All other platforms (desktop/mobile/web) match because their identifier and filename use the same form. verify-install.sh later warns: 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing'.

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: no script auto-advances .current_phase after Phase 1 steps complete; check-phase-gate.sh just reports 'Phase Gate Consistency Check / Current phase: 0 / Phase gates consistent.' I had to jq-edit .current_phase=2 manually to proceed with Section F.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing all PHASE1_STEPS via --complete-step phase1_architecture:*, run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh and inspect .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase
framework_bugs.expected: Some script advances .current_phase from 0/1 to 2 once Phase 1 gate passes
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase remained 0 after all 5 steps + gate check. Manual jq edit required to test downstream upgrade. Confirms U-D from 2026-04-25 sweep is still open.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: PR #17 U-F verified. Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json then ran upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc; exited RC=1 with [FAIL] upgrade blocked - pending user decision message.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: After clearing sentinel, upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc state mutations completed successfully (poc_mode advanced from private_poc to sponsored_poc, last_upgrade=2026-04-25 added, CLAUDE.md/PROJECT_INTAKE.md/APPROVAL_LOG.md updated, commit chore(upgrade): -> sponsored POC created), but the script exited RC=1 because the trailing 'Refreshing framework helper scripts (BL-009, BL-015)' block tried to cp scripts/pending-approval.sh onto itself. NOT a U-E regression: the poc_mode lookup from phase-state.json worked correctly. Distinct new bug, see framework_bugs[].

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: .current_phase remained 2; .project remained uat-agent-72; only .poc_mode and .last_upgrade changed (expected)

tests.name: process_state_preserved

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: Both project-local scripts/upgrade-project.sh and framework scripts/upgrade-project.sh headers reference 'BL-006 (2026-04-24)' and 'BL-015 (2026-04-25)' changelog entries.

tests.name: no_template_placeholders_in_output
tests.details: grep -E '__PROJECT_NAME__|__PLATFORM__|__LANGUAGE__|__TRACK__' on upgrade log returned 0 hits. (Note: this verifies the upgrade-project pathway does not exhibit U-I in this flow; U-I is still about init/upgrade template substitution and remains open separately.)

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh self-copy of pending-approval.sh causes RC=1 exit
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a project that was created via init.sh (which already copies scripts/pending-approval.sh into the project), run: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc
framework_bugs.expected: Upgrade exits 0 after applying state changes and refreshing helper scripts. Self-copy should be a no-op (cp src and dst are the same path when run from project's own scripts/).
framework_bugs.actual: Last operation prints 'cp: scripts/pending-approval.sh and /<project>/scripts/pending-approval.sh are identical (not copied).' and the script exits RC=1. SCRIPT_DIR (line 33) resolves to the PROJECT's scripts/ directory because the user invokes the project-local copy of upgrade-project.sh; the BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh block (lines 1471-1478) then attempts cp "$SCRIPT_DIR/$helper" "scripts/$helper" which is cp src dst with src==dst. macOS cp returns non-zero with 'are identical' error, propagating to script exit. All upstream state mutations did succeed; this is a tail-end bug. Workaround: ignore the non-zero exit and check phase-state.json directly. Fix: skip cp when src and dst resolve to the same canonical path, or sourcing helpers from $FRAMEWORK rather than from the project's own scripts/.

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/check-gate.sh referenced by upgrade-project.sh post-upgrade messages but not present in init.sh-generated projects
documentation_gaps.where: upgrade-project.sh emits '[INFO] bash scripts/check-gate.sh --preflight' and '[INFO] bash scripts/check-gate.sh --repair' after manifest backfill; the framework provides scripts/check-gate.sh but init.sh does not copy it into the project's scripts/ directory. Following the printed instructions yields 'No such file or directory'.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD == d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches start-of-run SHA). git status --short reported only pre-existing untracked dirs (.claude/, Reports/uat-2026-04-26/) which were present before this agent ran. No tracked-file modifications introduced by this run.

summary: private_poc->sponsored_poc upgrade on mcp_server/full mostly works (state correctly mutated, sentinel respected, history preserved), but upgrade-project.sh exits RC=1 due to a self-copy of scripts/pending-approval.sh in the BL-009/BL-015 helper-refresh block (High); also confirmed mcp_server platform-module filename mismatch (Medium) and U-D phase-advance gap (Medium).
